mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and reduce the present value of future equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean in >65% of high-rate observations, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid quarterly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >70% of instances where unemployment rises by more than 0.5 percentage points in three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in the residential real estate sector, boosting financial sector profitability and consumer confidence, which produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term yields a statistically insignificant coefficient (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production index values reflect robust manufacturing and utility output, which correlates with strong short-term corporate revenue generation and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO levels, or the signal produces negative returns in >60% of high-production observations.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds the 200 index threshold, elevated price levels erode real corporate margins and force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 200, or the inflation threshold shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of triggered months, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent quarter-over-quarter indicates a contraction in aggregate demand that reduces corporate profit forecasts and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in >70% of quarters where GDP growth drops below 1.0 percent, or the threshold shows no statistical significance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling real estate sales signals a shift in economic activity toward manufacturing and away from housing, which favors industrial sector earnings and generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces negative returns in >60% of instances, or the interaction term fails to improve predictive accuracy over single-series models in a 3-year rolling window.
